A.does: The Flask class implements a WSGI application and acts as the central object — a registry for view functions, URL rules, template configuration, and more.

B.does: The Blueprint class represents a collection of routes and other app-related functions that can be registered on a real application later, deferring the need for an application object by recording setup calls for later execution.

comparison: Blueprints attach to a Flask app via `app.register_blueprint(bp)`, which calls `Blueprint.register(app, options)` to replay all deferred setup functions — merging the blueprint's routes, error handlers, and request hooks into the live application.
